footerDisplay: Playback uses the [homebridge-homepod-radio](https://www.npmjs.com/package/homebridge-homepod-radio) `/play` route automatically when that plugin is installed; otherwise it streams directly with **pyatv** (`atvremote`). The LAN HTTP endpoint is **off by default** — only enable it on a trusted network (or set an access token).

name.title: Platform Name
name.description: Shown in the Homebridge log only.

phrase.title: Phrase to Speak
phrase.placeholder: Dinner is ready, come to the kitchen.

voice.title: Voice for this phrase (optional)
voice.description: Leave blank to use the default voice. A non-default voice is downloaded automatically the first time it speaks.

speed.title: Speed for this phrase (optional)
speed.description: Leave blank to use the default speed. 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = twice as fast, 0.5 = half speed.

atvId.title: Speaker for this phrase (optional)
atvId.description: Optional pyatv device ID to play this one phrase on a specific speaker/room. Leave blank to use the default playback target.

speed.title: Default Speech Speed
speed.description: 1.0 = normal, 2.0 = twice as fast, 0.5 = half speed. Applied to every phrase unless a button overrides it.

playback.title: Playback Target
playback.description: Auto-detects homebridge-homepod-radio and routes audio through it when installed; otherwise streams directly with pyatv. See the link at the bottom of this page.

atvId.title: pyatv Device ID
atvId.description: Identifier of the target speaker for pyatv (from `atvremote scan`). Required for the pyatv playback target.

voice.title: Voice
voice.description: Offline Piper voice. The voice you pick is downloaded automatically the first time it speaks. "Light" voices use the least memory — best on small boards.

oneOf.title: Amy - US English female (richer, more memory)

chimeFile.title: Attention Chime (optional)
chimeFile.description: Absolute path to a short WAV played right before each phrase. Leave blank for none.

enableHttp.title: Enable LAN HTTP "speak anything" endpoint
enableHttp.description: When on, exposes POST /say (and GET /say?text=...) so other devices on your network can speak arbitrary text. Keep it on a trusted LAN, and/or set an access token below.

httpToken.title: HTTP Access Token (optional)
httpToken.description: When set, every /say request must present this token via ?token=, an Authorization: Bearer header, or X-Auth-Token. /healthz stays open. Leave blank for no auth (trusted LAN only).

cacheEnabled.title: Cache synthesized phrases
cacheEnabled.description: Replay a repeated phrase from a cached WAV instead of re-synthesizing it. Strongly recommended on small boards — it removes the on-demand synthesis (and its memory spike) from the common path.

preRender.title: Pre-render phrases on startup
preRender.description: Synthesize and cache every button phrase once in the background at startup, so the first press is instant. Paced so it won't overload a small board. Requires the cache.

restoreVolume.title: Restore speaker volume after announcement (pyatv only)
restoreVolume.description: For the pyatv playback target, read the speaker's current volume before an announcement and restore it afterwards. Adds two extra pyatv calls per utterance.

cacheMaxEntries.title: Phrase Cache Size (entries)
cacheMaxEntries.description: Maximum number of cached phrase WAVs kept on disk (oldest are evicted first).

minAvailableMb.title: Minimum Free RAM (MiB) Before Synthesizing
minAvailableMb.description: Pre-flight memory gate. If MemAvailable is below this, the request is refused instead of risking an out-of-memory reboot on small boards. Set 0 to disable. (Cache hits are never gated.)

piperThreads.title: Piper Thread Cap
piperThreads.description: Optional. Fewer threads = lower CPU/memory spike, slower synthesis. Recommended: 1 on a single-core / low-RAM board.

enabled.title: Enable Soundboard
enabled.description: Expose a HomeKit Television whose inputs are the first audio files found in a folder. Selecting an input plays that sound on your speaker.

name.title: Soundboard Name
name.description: Name of the Television accessory shown in the Home app.

sourceFolder.title: Soundboard Source Folder
sourceFolder.description: Absolute path to a folder on the Pi. The first sounds found (depth-first) become the TV's inputs.

maxSounds.title: Maximum Inputs
maxSounds.description: How many sounds to expose as inputs (plus the synthetic 'None'). HomeKit limits a TV to a small number of inputs.

volume.title: Soundboard Volume (0-100)
volume.description: Optional volume for soundboard playback. Leave blank to use the default volume.

atvId.title: Soundboard Speaker (optional)
atvId.description: Optional pyatv device ID to play the soundboard on a specific speaker. Leave blank to use the default playback target.
